metadata.methodology_note: AAMM v3 pilot assessment based on collected GitHub API data. Scores computed from live data signals including file tree analysis, workflow inspection, AI config content review, PR/commit activity, and dependency scanning configuration. Branch protection returned 404 for all repos (insufficient API permissions) -- noted as a minimum viability risk, not a failure.

structural_clarity.evidence: Cargo workspace with [workspace] and multiple member crates. 1893 files, 509 dirs, max depth 9. 1001 Rust source files with median ~84 lines (excellent, <150 threshold). Multi-crate structure with clear module boundaries. Clean mod declarations. Rust R1 bonuses: workspace (+5), crate granularity (+3), module tree (+3), feature flags (+3), error types (+5) = 19, capped at +15. R1_raw ~93.75. R1 = min(100, 93.75+15) = 100, agent-adjusted to 90 (some inconsistency possible across 509 directories, 6% non-Rust code).

semantic_density.evidence: Rust is statically typed with full inference (type coverage=100). Serde derives with explicit attributes throughout. Doc comments (///) present on pub items, estimated 50-60% coverage. README with description, setup, usage, architecture sections (4/5 = 80). CHANGELOG present with structured format. ADRs in docs/ directory. No #![deny(missing_docs)]. Rust R2 bonuses: scoped unsafe minimal (+3), doc comments partial (+4), clippy (+5), serde attributes (+3) = 15, capped at +15. R2_raw ~83.75. Agent-adjusted to 87 (moderate doc ratio, no deny(missing_docs)).

verification_infra.evidence: 351 test files. Test/source ratio ~0.35 (0.2-0.4 range = score 50). Inline #[cfg(test)] modules + tests/ dirs (2 test categories). CI runs cargo test (ci.yml). No coverage tooling (cargo-tarpaulin or llvm-cov) detected. Build reproducibility: Cargo.lock committed + flake.nix + Dockerfile. Rust R3 bonuses: inline tests (+5), integration tests (+5), clippy in CI (+3) = 13. R3_raw ~62.75. R3 = min(100, 62.75+13) = 75.75, agent-adjusted to 76.

developer_ergonomics.evidence: rustfmt present, eslint + prettier for JS parts. deny.toml for cargo-deny. Makefile present. 19 workflow files (ci.yml, release.yml, pre-release.yml, etc.). No pre-commit hooks. No .editorconfig. Cargo.lock + flake.lock committed. Dockerfile + docker-compose present. Comprehensive .gitignore. Rust R4 bonuses: rustfmt (+3), deny.toml (+5) = 8. R4_raw ~74. R4 = min(100, 74+8) = 82.

code_quality.condition_a: Condition A met: rustfmt and clippy run in CI (ci.yml). PR review process active. eslint + prettier for JS/TS parts.
code_quality.condition_b: Condition B met: .github/copilot-instructions.md (158 lines, 7069 bytes) covers Rust error handling, import ordering, Mithril-specific conventions, and code review guidelines. Project-specific, substantive content.
code_quality.evidence: copilot-instructions.md covers coding conventions and architecture. Clippy + rustfmt enforced in CI. Sub-level Mid: config covers conventions AND architecture AND linter runs in CI. Not High: 0 AI co-authored commits, 0 AI bot PRs -- no emerging Stage 2 activity. Learning: static (single AI tool, no evidence of iterative refinement).

security.condition_a: Condition A partially met: deny.toml present for cargo-deny (dependency license/advisory checking). No Dependabot config. No Renovate. cargo-deny not confirmed running in CI workflows.
security.condition_b: Condition B not met: copilot-instructions.md does not contain security-specific content (no trust boundaries, no security-critical modules, no sensitive data flow documentation).
security.evidence: deny.toml present indicates security scanning awareness but cargo-deny not confirmed in CI. copilot-instructions.md lacks security context. Stage 0 Mid: practice partially active (deny.toml exists), no AI config for security. SECURITY.md present (disclosure policy).

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: cargo test runs in CI (ci.yml). 351 test files across crates. Inline #[cfg(test)] modules + tests/ directories.
testing.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md documents testing expectations for the project.
testing.evidence: Tests run in CI. copilot-instructions.md covers testing. Sub-level Low: minimal testing detail in AI config beyond basic standards. 0 AI test suggestions.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: release.yml and pre-release.yml workflows present in .github/workflows/. Automated release pipeline triggered on tags.
release.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md documents release-related conventions.
release.evidence: Release workflows present (release.yml, pre-release.yml). copilot-instructions.md covers release. Sub-level Low: basic release coverage in AI config.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: Condition A partially met: Dockerfile and docker-compose present (mithril has service components: aggregator, signer). Monitoring/alerting configuration not visible in repo.
ops_monitoring.condition_b: Condition B not met: copilot-instructions.md does not cover deployment topology, runbook locations, or escalation paths.
ops_monitoring.evidence: Mithril has service components (aggregator, signer) with Docker deployment but no monitoring config or AI ops context visible in repo. Stage 0 Low.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with 20 recent issues.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B met: copilot-instructions.md covers delivery workflow basics.
delivery.evidence: GitHub Issues active (20 recent). copilot-instructions.md covers delivery. Sub-level Low: minimal delivery detail in AI config. Confidence Medium: delivery tracking may be partially external.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A met: .github/copilot-instructions.md present with substantive content (158 lines, 7069 bytes -- well above 50-line quality threshold).
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B partially met: copilot-instructions.md implicitly documents AI usage expectations (code review guidelines section). No .aiignore or .cursorignore. Single AI tool configured.
ai_practices.evidence: Single AI tool (GitHub Copilot) configured with substantive instructions. No .aiignore. No multi-tool configuration. Sub-level Low: single tool caps at Mid per cross-pillar constraint, and Condition B only partially met so Low. Learning: static -- no evidence of iterative refinement of copilot-instructions.md.

repos.insight: Best single AI config in CBU pilot portfolio (copilot-instructions.md, 158 lines). 5 dimensions at Stage 1. Security at Stage 0 -- deny.toml exists but not confirmed in CI, and AI config lacks security context. Ops/Monitoring at Stage 0 -- service components exist but no monitoring config or AI ops context visible.

next_steps.action: Confirm cargo-deny runs in CI + add security trust boundaries to copilot-instructions.md (identify security-critical modules in mithril-stm and mithril-common, document cryptographic boundaries and sensitive data flows)
Add AI review bot (CodeRabbit or Claude) to PR workflow for automated code quality and security review
Update copilot-instructions.md based on team usage patterns + add .claude/commands/ for common workflows (test generation, release prep, crate scaffolding)

minimum_viability_risks: Branch protection: 404 (insufficient API permissions -- unable to verify PR review requirements)

structural_clarity.evidence: Multi-package cabal.project with 28 packages. 1648 files, 661 dirs, max depth 11. 1148 Haskell source files with median ~87 lines (excellent). 4-level module hierarchy. Exemplary multi-package structure. Explicit exports in >80% of modules (Haskell convention). Clear era-based separation of concerns. Internal module pattern used. Haskell R1 bonuses: module hierarchy (+5), explicit exports (+8), cabal structure (+5), Internal modules (+3), package boundaries (+5), typeclass discipline (+3) = 29, capped at +15. R1_raw ~96.25. Agent-adjusted to 95 (median file size above 84 lines but still excellent; some modules may be dense).

semantic_density.evidence: Fully typed Haskell with full inference (type coverage=100). CDDL specifications for ledger serialization formats. Type signatures on >90% of top-level bindings. Extensive newtype usage for domain concepts. DerivingStrategies extension. Refined types / smart constructors for core types. Haddock coverage 40-60% (varies by package). README covers 4/5 sections. CHANGELOG present. CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, .editorconfig, docs/ dir with ADRs. -Wall in most packages. Haskell R2 bonuses: -Wall -Werror (+8), Haddock partial (+4), type signatures (+10), newtypes (+5), DerivingStrategies (+3), smart constructors (+5) = 35, capped at +15. R2_raw ~88.5. Agent-adjusted to 92 (Haddock coverage moderate, not all packages equally documented).

verification_infra.evidence: 755 test files. Test/source ratio ~0.66 (good, 0.4-0.7 range). 3 test categories: unit, property-based (QuickCheck/Hedgehog), golden tests (tasty-golden). Tasty framework. CI runs cabal test on every PR (haskell.yml), tests block merge. Nix flake for fully reproducible builds. Coverage partially configured but not enforced. No doctest. Haskell R3 bonuses: test-suite stanza (+5), Tasty (+5), QuickCheck/Hedgehog (+8), hpc partial (+1), cabal test in CI (+3), golden tests (+3) = 25, capped at +15. R3_raw ~82.75. Agent-adjusted to 88 (coverage not enforced).

developer_ergonomics.evidence: fourmolu/ormolu formatter in CI. No dedicated hlint.yaml file found (may run in CI). .editorconfig present. hie.yaml for HLS/IDE support. Nix flake with devShell (gold standard reproducibility). 4 workflow files (haskell.yml, bench.yml, gh-pages.yml, push-specs.yml). cabal.project with 28 packages. flake.lock committed. No pre-commit hooks. No Makefile, no Dockerfile. Haskell R4 bonuses: fourmolu (+3), cabal.project (+3), hie.yaml (+3), Nix flake (+8) = 17, capped at +15. R4_raw ~74. Agent-adjusted to 85 (no pre-commit hooks, limited task runner).

code_quality.condition_a: Condition A met: fourmolu/ormolu formatter enforced in CI. PR review process active.
code_quality.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present (zero AI configuration -- no CLAUDE.md, no copilot-instructions.md, no .cursorrules, no .mcp.json).
code_quality.evidence: Excellent engineering practices (fourmolu, CI enforcement, PR reviews) but zero AI configuration anywhere in the repo. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A fully met, Condition B not met.

security.condition_a: Condition A partially met: No Dependabot config file, no Renovate. However, 1 Dependabot bot PR detected (bot is active at org level). SECURITY.md present for disclosure.
security.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
security.evidence: Dependabot bot PR detected (1 recent) indicating org-level Dependabot activity, but no .github/dependabot.yml config file in repo. SECURITY.md present. No AI config for security context. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A partially met (Dependabot active at org level), Condition B not met.

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: cabal test runs in CI on every PR (haskell.yml). 755 test files across 28 packages. QuickCheck, Hedgehog, Tasty, golden tests.
testing.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
testing.evidence: World-class test infrastructure (property-based testing, golden tests, CI-enforced, test/source ratio 0.66) but zero AI configuration. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A fully met, Condition B not met.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: haskell.yml CI workflow with build jobs. gh-pages.yml for documentation publishing.
release.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
release.evidence: CI build workflows exist. CHANGELOG present. No AI config covering release process. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: Condition A not met: cardano-ledger is primarily a library (ledger rules implementation). No deployment infrastructure, no monitoring config, no alerting visible. Used by cardano-node but not deployed as a service itself.
ops_monitoring.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
ops_monitoring.evidence: Library repo -- ledger rules used by cardano-node but cardano-ledger itself is not a deployed service. Stage 0 Low (n/a -- library). Weight not redistributed as repo contains executable test runners and bench components.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with 20 recent issues.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
delivery.evidence: Issue tracking active on GitHub (20 recent issues). No AI config covering delivery workflow. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A not met: Zero AI config files present in the repository.
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI usage expectations documented.
ai_practices.evidence: No AI tooling configured whatsoever. Stage 0 (blank): neither condition met. This is not a gap -- it reflects that the team has not yet institutionalized AI tools. The codebase is world-class engineering (Readiness 91) ready for AI investment.

repos.insight: World-class Haskell engineering (Readiness 91) with zero AI adoption. All SDLC practices are active -- the only missing piece is AI configuration. Highest ROI for AI investment in CBU. A single CLAUDE.md would advance 5 dimensions from Stage 0 to Stage 1 simultaneously.

next_steps.action: Add CLAUDE.md covering the 28-package architecture (era-based structure), coding conventions (Haskell patterns, newtype discipline, DerivingStrategies), test standards (Tasty, QuickCheck, Hedgehog, golden tests), release process, and delivery workflow

Add security context to CLAUDE.md (trust boundaries in ledger rule validation, cryptographic verification modules, sensitive data flows in transaction processing) + configure Dependabot for Haskell deps
Add AI review bot (CodeRabbit or Claude) to PR workflow for automated code quality review across the 28 packages

structural_clarity.evidence: cabal.project with 12 packages. 900 files, 270 dirs, max depth 7. 317 Haskell source files with median ~70 lines (excellent, well under 150). Multi-package structure with clear module boundaries. Clean separation of concerns across packages. Haskell R1 bonuses: module hierarchy (+5), explicit exports (+8), cabal structure (+5), Internal modules (+3), package boundaries (+5), typeclass discipline (+3) = 29, capped at +15. R1_raw ~96.25. Agent-adjusted to 92 (smaller project than ledger, excellent granularity, fewer packages).

semantic_density.evidence: Fully typed Haskell (type coverage=100). Some schema work for Hydra protocol but less formal than ledger's CDDL. Haddock documentation coverage estimated 40-50%. README with description, setup, architecture (4/5 sections = 80). CHANGELOG present. CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, .editorconfig, docs/ dir with ADRs. Haskell R2 bonuses: -Wall partial (+5), Haddock partial (+4), type signatures (+10), newtypes (+5), DerivingStrategies (+3) = 27, capped at +15. R2_raw ~76.75. Agent-adjusted to 80 (less formal schemas than ledger, moderate Haddock coverage).

verification_infra.evidence: 157 test files. Test/source ratio ~0.50 (0.4-0.7 range = 75). 3+ test categories: unit, property-based (QuickCheck/Hedgehog likely), integration (hydra-cluster network tests). Dedicated hydra-test-utils package for test infrastructure. CI runs cabal test (ci-nix.yaml), smoke-test.yaml, network-test.yaml, nightly-ci.yaml. Nix flake for reproducible builds. No coverage enforcement. Haskell R3 bonuses: test-suite stanza (+5), HSpec/Tasty (+5), QuickCheck/Hedgehog (+8), cabal test CI (+3) = 21, capped at +15. R3_raw ~83.75. Agent-adjusted to 84 (no coverage config, but excellent test infrastructure with dedicated test utils package).

developer_ergonomics.evidence: hlint (hlint.yaml present). fourmolu formatter. .editorconfig present. hie.yaml for HLS/IDE support. Nix flake with devShell. 12 workflow files (ci-nix.yaml, docker.yaml, smoke-test.yaml, etc.). docker-compose present. flake.lock committed. No pre-commit hooks. No Makefile. Haskell R4 bonuses: fourmolu (+3), hlint (+5), cabal.project (+3), hie.yaml (+3), Nix flake (+8) = 22, capped at +15. R4_raw ~76.5. Agent-adjusted to 87 (excellent editor/IDE support with .editorconfig + hie.yaml, comprehensive CI with 12 workflows).

code_quality.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present (zero AI configuration).

infrastructure_readiness: Dependabot active at org level (2 bot PRs detected)

structural_clarity.evidence: Multi-package monorepo: 11 package.json files. 3667 files, 759 dirs, max depth 14. 2400 TS/TSX source files with median ~32 lines (excellent). Multiple tsconfig.json files per package. Deep nesting with clear package structure. TypeScript R1 bonuses: path aliases likely (+5), barrel files likely (+3), monorepo workspace structure (+5) = 13. R1_raw ~87.5. Agent-adjusted to 82 (browser extension architecture may mix concerns; deep nesting at depth 14 could indicate some structural complexity).

semantic_density.evidence: TypeScript 87% -- strict mode status unknown. Estimated type coverage score 65 (typed but strict mode unconfirmed). No formal schema definitions documented (wallet app likely has some runtime validation). Doc comments estimated sparse (<20%). README present but no CHANGELOG, no SECURITY.md, no CONTRIBUTING.md. No docs/ dir, no ADRs. TypeScript R2 bonuses: estimated +5 (likely type-only imports and generics). R2_raw ~39.5. Agent-adjusted to 48 (large TypeScript monorepo with types provides baseline semantic value, but severe documentation gaps).

verification_infra.evidence: 857 test files. Test/source ratio ~0.36 (0.2-0.4 range). jest/vitest config present. E2E tests: e2e-tests-linux-split.yml workflow. sonar-cloud.yml suggests some coverage analysis. CI runs tests (ci.yml). Build reproducibility: flake.nix + flake.lock. TypeScript R3 bonuses: jest/vitest config (+5), *.test.ts convention (+3), E2E testing (+5), package.json test script (+3) = 16, capped at +15. R3_raw ~72.75. Agent-adjusted to 78 (test ratio below 0.4, but E2E testing present and SonarCloud configured).

developer_ergonomics.evidence: eslint and prettier configured. 9 workflow files (ci.yml, e2e-tests-linux-split.yml, release-lace.yml, sonar-cloud.yml, etc.). Nix flake present (flake.nix + flake.lock). Makefile present. No pre-commit hooks. No .editorconfig. No CONTRIBUTING.md. package-lock/yarn.lock committed. TypeScript R4 bonuses: prettier (+3), eslint (+3) = 6. R4_raw ~74. Agent-adjusted to 73 (no pre-commit hooks, no editorconfig, no env template, no CONTRIBUTING.md).

code_quality.condition_a: Condition A met: eslint and prettier configured. CI runs quality checks (ci.yml). PR review process active.
code_quality.condition_b: Condition B not met: .mcp.json contains only MCP server definitions (3 servers: sequential-thinking, context7, interactive). No coding conventions, architecture, or module boundaries documented for AI tools.
code_quality.evidence: eslint + prettier active in CI. .mcp.json shows AI tool awareness (3 MCP servers) but provides no project context for coding conventions. Stage 0 High: Condition A fully met + Condition B has emerging signals (.mcp.json demonstrates AI tool usage intent).

security.condition_a: Condition A partially met: .github/dependabot.yml present but likely covers only github-actions scope, not npm packages.
security.condition_b: Condition B not met: .mcp.json contains no security context.
security.evidence: Dependabot configured (github-actions scope likely). .mcp.json shows AI tool awareness but no security context. Stage 0 High: Condition A partially met (Dependabot present, scope limited) + AI tool awareness via .mcp.json.

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: jest/vitest configured. 857 test files. E2E tests (e2e-tests-linux-split.yml). CI runs tests.
testing.condition_b: Condition B not met: .mcp.json contains no testing standards or coverage expectations.
testing.evidence: Comprehensive test infrastructure (857 test files, jest/vitest, E2E). .mcp.json shows AI tool awareness. Stage 0 High: Condition A fully met + emerging AI signals.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: release-lace.yml workflow present. CI builds and releases.
release.condition_b: Condition B not met: .mcp.json contains no release process documentation.
release.evidence: Release workflow exists (release-lace.yml). .mcp.json shows AI tool awareness. Stage 0 High: Condition A met + emerging AI signals.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: N/A: Lace is a browser extension (Cardano wallet). No server-side production deployment.
ops_monitoring.evidence: Browser extension -- no server-side production. Stage 0 (n/a -- not a service). Ops weight (0.10) redistributed proportionally to remaining dimensions.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with 20 recent issues.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B not met: .mcp.json contains no delivery workflow documentation.
delivery.evidence: Issue tracking active (20 recent issues). .mcp.json shows AI tool awareness. Stage 0 High: Condition A met + emerging AI signals.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A partially met: .mcp.json (27 lines, 547 bytes) configures 3 MCP servers (sequential-thinking, context7, interactive). AI tool is configured but content is tool configuration only, not substantive project context. Uses npx -y (unpinned versions).
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI usage expectations documented. No .aiignore or .cursorignore. .mcp.json provides no guidance on when/how to use AI or attribution expectations.
ai_practices.evidence: .mcp.json with 3 MCP servers shows AI tool awareness and active configuration. But lacks substantive project context and usage expectations. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A partially met (tool configured but thin content). Learning: static (no evidence of iterative refinement).

repos.insight: All SDLC practices active (Stage 0 High on 5 dimensions) + MCP config shows AI tool usage. But .mcp.json does not provide project context -- no coding conventions, test standards, or architecture documented for AI tools. Adding CLAUDE.md would unlock Stage 1 on 5 dimensions simultaneously. R2 Semantic Density (48) is the weakest foundation in the pilot portfolio -- missing CHANGELOG, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, ADRs.

next_steps.action: Add CLAUDE.md with architecture (11-package monorepo, browser extension structure), coding standards, test conventions (jest/vitest, E2E patterns), release process, delivery workflow + pin MCP server versions in .mcp.json
Add security trust boundaries to CLAUDE.md (wallet key management, transaction signing, dApp connector trust model) + expand Dependabot to cover npm packages
Improve R2 Semantic Density: add CHANGELOG.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, enable strict mode in tsconfig if not already, add ADRs for key architectural decisions

impact.detail: R2 improvement from ~48 to ~72 would lift Readiness from 69 to ~77

testing.condition_a: Condition A met: cabal test runs in CI (haskell.yml). 370 test files. Test/source ratio 0.73 (excellent). Property-based testing likely present.
testing.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
testing.evidence: Excellent test infrastructure with 0.73 test/source ratio. Zero AI configuration. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A fully met, Condition B not met.

release.condition_a: Condition A met: release-ghcr.yaml and release-upload.yaml workflows present. Automated release pipeline for container images and artifacts.
release.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
release.evidence: Release workflows present (release-ghcr.yaml, release-upload.yaml). CHANGELOG present with check-changelog.yml enforcing changelog updates. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ops_monitoring.condition_a: Condition A partially met: docker-compose present (node is a deployed service). But no monitoring config, alerting rules, or runbooks visible in the repo.
ops_monitoring.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
ops_monitoring.evidence: cardano-node is a deployed service (not a library) with docker-compose, but monitoring/alerting configuration not visible in the repo. Monitoring may exist externally. Stage 0 Low.

delivery.condition_a: Condition A met: GitHub Issues active with 20 recent issues.
delivery.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI config files present.
delivery.evidence: Issue tracking active (20 recent issues). No AI config covering delivery workflow. Stage 0 Mid: Condition A met, Condition B not met.

ai_practices.condition_a: Condition A not met: Zero AI config files present in the repository.
ai_practices.condition_b: Condition B not met: No AI usage expectations documented.
ai_practices.evidence: No AI tooling configured. Stage 0 (blank): neither condition met.

repos.insight: Core Cardano infrastructure (Readiness 84) with zero AI adoption. Strong verification infrastructure (test/source ratio 0.73, comprehensive CI with 14 workflows including dedicated check-hlint.yml and check-changelog.yml). Security is the weakest area -- no automated dependency scanning despite being critical blockchain infrastructure. Adding CLAUDE.md + Dependabot would be highest-leverage actions.

next_steps.action: Add CLAUDE.md covering the 15-package architecture (node, CLI, API, submit-api), coding conventions, test standards, release process (GHCR, artifact upload), and delivery workflow
Enable Dependabot for Haskell deps + add security context to CLAUDE.md (consensus validation trust boundaries, cryptographic modules, network protocol security, key management)
Add AI review bot (CodeRabbit or Claude) to PR workflow for automated code quality review across the 15 packages

flags: No automated dependency scanning for critical blockchain node infrastructure
minimum_viability_risks: Branch protection: 404 (insufficient API permissions -- unable to verify PR review requirements)

org_summary.headline: All 5 pilot repos are Fertile Ground -- strong engineering foundations (avg Readiness 83) with minimal AI adoption (avg Adoption 8). Only mithril has any AI config (copilot-instructions.md). The CBU's primary opportunity is activation, not remediation. Single highest-leverage action: add CLAUDE.md to the 4 repos that don't have one, which would advance 4-5 dimensions per repo from Stage 0 to Stage 1.
